data.title: Nexstar to replace local network news with NewsNation content -- Bloomberg
data.description:
Nexstar (NXST) will no longer use segments from national networks like ABC (DIS), NBC (CMCSA), and CBS (PSKY) during its local newscasts and will instead use content from its own NewsNation cable channel instead.

According to sources cited by Bloomberg, Nexstar, the largest owner of local TV stations in the U.S., ended its agreement with NBC (CMCSA) to share nationally reported segments and will allow remaining agreements with other broadcast networks to terminate as they expire.

In a letter to the chairman of the Federal Communications Commission, Brendan Carr, regarding the pending merger with TENGA, Nexstar CEO Perry Sook telegraphed his company’s intention to end national broadcast agreements and “expand its investment in local news and programming after its acquisition of TENGA is complete,” adding that Nexstar (NXST) remains dedicated to “preserving the independence of our local newsroom at a time when national media organizations increasingly dictate the news agenda.”

“We remain committed to operating both our existing stations and those we will acquire from TENGA in a manner that serves the public interest and strengthens the local communities they serve,” Sook said in his letter.

TENGA stations have a news licensing agreement with CNN (WBD), currently part of a merger agreement with Paramount Skydance (PSKY).

Although the $3.5B Nexstar/TENGA merger was approved, a California federal judge halted the integration while an antitrust lawsuit led by a coalition of state attorneys general and DirectTV (TPG) is resolved. The lawsuit claims that the merger will impede competition for broadcast TV and result in higher fees.

Nexstar (NXST) shares are down more than 7% into Monday’s open.

data.title: Virtus Investment Partners sees AUM of $149B as of March end
data.description:
Virtus Investment Partners (VRTS) said on Monday that its preliminary AUM as of March 31 stood at $149.0B, while other fee-earning assets came in at $1.6B for total client assets of $150.6B.

The figure compares with preliminary AUM of $155.9B and other fee-earning assets of $1.7B for total client assets of $157.6B as of February 28.

Notably, the S&P 500 Index declined by 5.09% during March to close at 6,528.47 points.

Virtus Investment's March AUM by product type is as follows - Open-End Funds: $50.2B, Closed-End Funds: $13.1B, Retail Separate Accounts: $37.3B, and Institutional Accounts: $48.3B.

By asset class, Equity accounted for $70.1B of the total AUM, Fixed Income for $39.4B, Multi-Asset for $21.6B, and Alternatives for $18.0B.

data.title: DLT Resolution expands into life settlements through LifeChain Financial
data.description:
• DLT Resolution (DLTI) said on Monday that its unit LifeChain Financial is entering the U.S. life settlements market to acquire and manage discounted portfolios of highly rated life insurance policies to maturity.
  • The company said life settlements are a non-correlated alternative asset class offering attractive risk-adjusted returns.
  • The market’s annual potential is estimated at $224B, with transaction volumes expected to reach around $4.6B, supported by demographic tailwinds and rising policy lapse rates.
  • Shares +1.18%.

data.title: Regeneron inks deal with Telix for radiopharmaceutical therapies
data.description:
• Regeneron Pharmaceuticals (REGN) and Telix Pharmaceuticals (TLX) will work together to develop next-generation radiopharmaceutical therapies under a 50/50 cost- and profit-sharing collaboration.
  • Terms call for Telix to receive an upfront payment of $40M, giving Regeneron access to that company's radiopharmaceutical manufacturing platform for four initial therapeutic programs. Regeneron also has an option for four more programs along with upfront payments. 
  • If Telix decides to opt out of a co-funding model for an individual program, it is still eligible to receive up to $535M in development and commercial milestones, as well as low double-digit royalties on future sales.
  • The two companies will also jointly work on diagnostic programs. Telix will head commercialization, with Regeneron getting a part of the profits.

data.title: AdaptHealth refinances $1.1B senior secured credit facility
data.description:
• AdaptHealth (AHCO) said on Monday it had closed a $1.1B senior secured credit facility, comprising a $325M term loan A, a $325M delayed draw term loan, and a $450M revolving credit facility.
  • The company plans to use proceeds from the new $325M term loan to fully repay its existing term loan without penalty.
  • The $450M revolving credit will replace the company's prior $300M revolving credit facility, which had $100M outstanding at closing, and provides greater liquidity to support ongoing operations.
  • The delayed draw facility of $325M provides access to committed capital in two potential draws over one year, intended for redemption of the company's 6.125% senior notes due 2028 upon becoming callable at par in August 2026, reducing financing costs.
  • The new credit facility features lower pricing, with the minimum interest rate reduced to 1.125% over SOFR and now linked to the company's leverage ratio, enabling further cost reductions with deleveraging.
  • The facility matures in April 2031, extending debt maturities by about two years, and is expected to lower the company's weighted average cost of debt by at least 25 bps following redemption of its 2028 senior notes.

data.title: Lilly Jaypirca data shows extended progression-free survival in CLL
data.description:
• New phase 3 data from Eli Lilly (LLY) demonstrated that its non-covalent (reversible) Bruton tyrosine kinase (BTK) inhibitor, Jaypirca (pirtobrutinib), plus Venclexta (venetoclax) and rituximab, led to better progression-free survival than Venclexta and rituximab alone in patients with chronic lymphocytic leukemia or small lymphocytic lymphoma.
  • Patients in both arms were given treatment for up to two years.
  • The drugmaker said that results were the same whether or not a patient was previously treated with a covalent BTK inhibitor.
  • Data on a secondary endpoint, overall survival, was not yet mature but was trending in favor of the Jayprica-treated cohort.
  • Lilly said it would provide the data to the US FDA and other regulators later this year for a label expansion.
  • Jaypirca is also approved for mantle cell lymphoma. All current indications are for patients who have failed on a BTK inhibitor.

data.title: AeroVironment appoints Sean Woodward as CFO
data.description:
• AeroVironment (AVAV) announced on Monday the appointment of Sean T. Woodward as executive vice president and chief financial officer, effective May 1, 2026.
  • Woodward succeeds Kevin McDonnell, who will be stepping down from the role, as announced earlier this year. McDonnell will remain with the company in an advisory role through July 2026 to help ensure a smooth transition of responsibilities, the company said. 
  • The company added that Woodward joined AV in 2010 and has spent more than 15 years in leadership roles across the company, supporting its revenue growth and profitability that led AV to becoming a global defense technology leader with roughly $10B in market cap. Woodward most recently served as CFO of AV’s Autonomous Systems segment.
  • AVAV -1.33% premarket to $177.33.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Rectitude Holdings secures over S$10M in AIMS contract orders
data.description:
• Rectitude Holdings (RECT), a Singapore-based provider of safety equipment and related industrial products, on Monday said it has secured more than S$10 million in rental and sales contract orders during the second half of fiscal year 2026.
  • "The surge in demand is supported by a robust construction pipeline, favorable pro–green energy policies, and rising global oil prices driven by recent geopolitical tensions in the Middle East," the company said.
  • The company has observed a significant acceleration in order volume for the All-in-One Intelligence Micro-Grid System ((AIMS)) in recent months and expects this strong momentum may continue throughout the remainder of the year, it added.
  • RECT +65.8% premarket to $1.99.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: On Semiconductor gets an upgrade, NXP's rating cut at BofA
data.description:
BofA upgraded On Semiconductor (ON) to Buy and downgraded NXP Semiconductors (NXPI) to Neutral, while raising the price targets on Analog Devices (ADI) and Macom Technology (MTSI).

ON Semiconductor (ON)

The firm upgraded On Semiconductor's stock to Buy from Neutral and increased the price target to $85 from $70. Shares of the company jumped about 3% premarket on Monday.

"The slow auto/EV environment makes our ON upgrade potentially a tad early, but we like the company’s 1) pipeline (rising AI power, Treo products), 2) solid FCF [free cash flow] generation (~6% FCF yield), 3) buyback commitment ($6bn/next 3 years, essentially 100% of FCF), 4) model leverage (EBIT margins could reach 1.5x to ~30% by CY28E), and 5) and low balance sheet leverage (dry power for potential M&A, as discussed by management)," said analysts led by Vivek Arya.

The analyst added that there is catch-up potential, with the stock down 36% in the past three years on auto/EV/Tesla-related weakness. They noted that On Semiconductor has responded well by pruning its portfolio, cutting costs, and focusing on FCF generation/returns. The company has also invested in boosting its AI power exposure, likely a focus at the upcoming analyst day in September, a potentially positive catalyst, the analysts added.

NXP Semiconductors (NXPI) 

BofA downgraded shares of NXP to Neutral from Buy and cut its price target to $230 from $245. Shares of NXP fell nearly 3% premarket.

The analysts said they like NXP's strength in high-value advanced driver assist systems, or ADAS, radar, and zonal processors. The company managed the prior downcycle very well, as reflected innearly unanimous (over 85%) Buy ratings, according to the analysts.

"However, the downside of managing the cycle well is limited earnings leverage (only 300-400bps expansion modeled from CY25-28E, though management could raise target model). We further note lack of AI products, limited EV leverage, planned exit from (high margin) communications infrastructure/RF, and higher exposure to consumer phones/IoT that could be pressured by constrained memory supply," said Arya and his team.

Analog Devices and Macom

The analysts maintained their Buy ratings on Analog Devices and Macom. They raised Analog Devices' price target to $425 from $400 and Macom's price objective to $305 from $260.

data.title: Freehold Royalties announces departure of CFO Shaina Morihira
data.description:
• Freehold Royalties (FRU:CA) announced on Monday that Shaina Morihira has submitted her resignation as vice president, finance, and chief financial officer, effective May 15, 2026.
  • Morihira will remain with the company until May 15, 2026, to ensure a smooth transition of responsibilities, including the filing of the company's Q1 2026 financial results on May 12, 2026.
  • Meanwhile, the company has initiated a formal search for Morihira’s successor through the assistance of an executive search firm. 
  • FRHLF +0.03% after hours to $12.3934.
  • Source: Press Release
